sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Borussia Dortmund, currently 5th in the Bundesliga with 51 points, face off against VfL Wolfsburg, who sit in 12th place with 39 points. This Round 32 clash at Signal Iduna Park in Dortmund promises an intriguing battle. Dortmund have been in fine form with 3 consecutive wins and an unbeaten streak of 4 matches. In contrast, Wolfsburg are struggling, having failed to win in their last 7 matches. Historically, Dortmund have dominated recent head-to-heads, winning 3 of the last 5 encounters, with 1 draw and 1 win for Wolfsburg. The last meeting on December 22, 2024, saw Dortmund triumph 3-1 away, with Julian Brandt earning Player of the Match honors with an 8.5 Sofascore rating.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Dortmund's attacking prowess is evident, with 7 of their last 7 home matches producing over 2.5 goals. They've also been the first to score in 5 of their last 7 games and have won the first half in 5 of those matches as well. However, defensive frailties remain, as they’ve failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 11 home games. Wolfsburg, on the other hand, have been leaky at the back, conceding first in 6 of their last 7 matches and going 11 games without a clean sheet away from home. Their matches tend to be low-scoring recently, with 5 of their last 7 games seeing under 2.5 goals.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Borussia Dortmund
sections.text: Dortmund boast several standout players, including Serhou Guirassy, who ranks 2nd in the league for goals scored with 19 from an expected goals (xG) tally of 21.29. Guirassy has been in scintillating form, netting 5 goals in his last 5 matches with a Sofascore rating of 7.28. Jamie Gittens, a midfielder with 8 goals and a league rank of 4 for successful dribbles per game (2.6), is another danger man. Julian Brandt, with 8 assists and ranking 4th in the league for big chances created (15), adds creativity, while defender Niklas Süle stands out defensively, ranking 3rd league-wide for aerial duels won (70.6%). Keep an eye on Daniel Svensson too, who has impressed recently with a 7.525 Sofascore rating over his last 5 matches.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - VfL Wolfsburg
sections.text: For Wolfsburg, Mohamed El Amine Amoura leads the attack with 10 goals and 8 assists, showcasing his ability to outperform his xG of 8.66. Jonas Wind, another forward, has contributed 8 goals and 3 assists, exceeding his xG of 5.34. Defender Denis Vavro offers a threat from the back, ranking 4th in the league for long balls as a percentage of total passes (10%). Midfielder Patrick Wimmer and defender Joakim Mæhle add dynamism with 3 goals each and a combined 8 assists, though Wolfsburg lack players in form based on recent matches.

sections.subtitle: Missing Players Impact
sections.text: Dortmund will feel the absence of key players like Pascal Groß, who ranks 4th in the league for assists with 9, and forward Maximilian Beier, who has 8 goals this season. Defender Nico Schlotterbeck and midfielder Marcel Sabitzer are also sidelined, which could impact their defensive stability and midfield control. Wolfsburg are without midfielder Maximilian Arnold, who has 3 goals and 5 assists, alongside Mattias Svanberg and defender Sebastiaan Bornauw, potentially weakening their midfield and backline further.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Stats
sections.text: Dortmund’s season stats highlight their attacking dominance, ranking 3rd in the league for total goals scored (64), ball possession (59.41%), and corners per game (5.75). They also lead the league in percentage of goals assisted (78.13%) and rank 2nd for aerial duels won (53.85%). However, their defense ranks poorly, conceding 49 goals (9th best) and missing 58 big chances (14th worst). Wolfsburg’s goal distribution shows they are most dangerous late in games, with 17.31% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes. Dortmund, conversely, peak between the 16th and 30th minutes, scoring 24.49% of their goals in that period.

sections.subtitle: Referee and Discipline
sections.text: Felix Brych, the referee for this match, averages 4.0 yellow cards per game and 0.19 red cards per game, suggesting a moderate likelihood of bookings. Dortmund have accumulated 63 yellow cards this season, averaging 1.97 per game, ranking 7th for least yellows in the league. Recent trends show 6 of Dortmund’s last 7 matches have seen under 4.5 cards, and head-to-head data indicates 4 of the last 5 matches between these teams also stayed under that threshold. Expect a relatively disciplined game unless tensions flare.

sections.subtitle: Historical Context and Interesting Facts
sections.text: In head-to-head matchups, Dortmund have been the first to score in 4 of the last 5 games against Wolfsburg, a trend that aligns with their recent form of scoring first in 5 of their last 7 matches. Serhou Guirassy has been a standout in past encounters with Wolfsburg, averaging an 8.04 Sofascore rating over the last 5 matchups, scoring 7 goals with no assists in just 2 starts. Dortmund’s attacking output at home, combined with Wolfsburg’s inability to keep clean sheets away (11 straight games), points to a potential goal-fest. Signal Iduna Park has been a fortress for Dortmund, and with 101 big chances created this season (4th in the league), they are likely to test Wolfsburg’s shaky defense.
